exploration_log.aha_moment: The `store` handler's `default` case (covering embedded tag values 6-20) was silently calling `parse_cbor_internal` instead of rejecting. For numeric-subtype tags (0xD8-0xDB), the decoded value needed to be tracked in a common variable to enable post-read range validation.

dead_ends.explored: parse_cbor_internal function structure to understand full tag processing flow
dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Only the cbor_tag_handler_t::store path needed fixing; the error and ignore handlers were out of scope

exploration_log.hindsight: Could have immediately focused on the `store` handler's two failure modes (embedded tags and out-of-range numeric tags) without tracing the full parsing pipeline first.

alternatives: Validate only in the 0xD8 case (1-byte tag)
Validate in all cases after reading the tag value
key_decisions.decision: Track decoded tag value in a common uint64_t and validate after the switch
key_decisions.reasoning: Handles all encoding widths (0xD8-0xDB) uniformly with a single check; also fixes the default case for embedded tags 6-20

solve_notes: In the `cbor_tag_handler_t::store` case inside `parse_cbor_internal`, the `default` branch (covering embedded tag values 6-20 from bytes 0xC6-0xD4) silently called `parse_cbor_internal` instead of rejecting. For the numeric-width tag cases (0xD8-0xDB), no range check existed. I added a common `tag_value` variable that captures the decoded tag integer, changed the `default` branch to throw `parse_error.112`, and added a post-switch range check that rejects values outside 64-87.
task_text:
Fix binary_reader not validating CBOR tag values for typed arrays

The CBOR reader in `detail/input/binary_reader.hpp` accepts any tag
value without validation when reading typed arrays (tags 64–87).
Invalid tag values outside the typed-array range are silently treated
as untagged data. Fix the reader to reject unexpected tag values with
`parse_error.112`.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: cbor_tag_handler_t
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: the three handler modes (error, ignore, store) determine which branch needs fixing; only store handles typed arrays

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just `parse_cbor_internal` (the edited function). The thrash_preventing set adds `cbor_tag_handler_t` to clarify that only the `store` mode is affected by this fix — without it, an agent might incorrectly try to add validation to the `ignore` or `error` paths.
